title: Strategies for Improving Antibiotic Use in Outpatient Hemodialysis Facilities | Antibiotic Prescribing and Use | CDC
text:

Provide education on antibiotic use 
Clinician education is critical to any antibiotic stewardship activity. However, education should be paired with other interventions to improve prescribing practices. Education topics should include: early sepsis prevention and management, antibiotic selection and dosing for empiric treatment of common infections, blood culture collection procedures, and antibiotic adjustment based on microbiology data.
Combine antibiotic stewardship practices with other quality improvement initiatives 
Outpatient dialysis facilities use existing structures and programs to assess quality measures and conduct quality improvement initiatives. Facilities are incentivized by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to report bloodstream infections to NHSN. They are mandated by CMS to have monthly Quality Assessment and Performance Improvement (QAPI) program meetings where the medical director and clinical staff review specific health outcome measures, such as bloodstream infection rates.
Resources and tools 
Did you know?‎ 
CDC and partners published a white paper, Opportunities to Improve Antibiotic Prescribing in Outpatient Hemodialysis Facilities, highlighting opportunities and strategies to optimize antibiotic use in outpatient hemodialysis facilities.
